title: Complexity, Timing, Profiling, Memory, and Capacity · Behavior Case: Memory Profile
summary: A team optimizes string concatenation from one warm run, reports elapsed time without input size, never measures memory, and ships a faster tokenizer that changes word policy. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the memory profile increment in correctness-preserving capacity study. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Measure peak allocations or process memory for batch and stream paths against vocabulary and output growth.
Use cProfile or a justified profiler to locate cumulative work before selecting a repair.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-memory-profile — Measure peak allocations or process memory for batch and stream paths against vocabulary and output growth.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-performance-profiling-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-performance-profiling-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_performance_profiling_5(tokens: int = 10000, elapsed_ms: float = 42.0, peak_kib: int = 640, time_budget_ms: float = 100.0, memory_budget_kib: int = 1024, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or elapsed_ms < 0.0 or peak_kib < 0:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "measurement-shape"
    if elapsed_ms > time_budget_ms:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "time-budget"
    if peak_kib > memory_budget_kib:
        return False, peak_kib, "memory-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert elapsed_ms <= time_budget_ms
    return True, round(elapsed_ms, 3), "profiled-capacity"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-memory-profile-17qcba
# Competency: bookbot-memory-profile.
# Case 2792: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_memory_profile_17qcba(tokens: int = 10000, elapsed_ms: float = 42.0, peak_kib: int = 640, time_budget_ms: float = 100.0, memory_budget_kib: int = 1024, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17qcba = "17qcba-abcq71-2792"
    assert evidence_variant_17qcba
    evidence_axes_17qcba = (
        "corpus17qcba",
        "policyabcq71",
        "revision2792",
        "command17qcba2792",
        "reportabcq712792",
        "test279217qcba",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17qcba)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or elapsed_ms < 0.0 or peak_kib < 0:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "measurement-shape"
    if elapsed_ms > time_budget_ms:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "time-budget"
    if peak_kib > memory_budget_kib:
        return False, peak_kib, "memory-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert elapsed_ms <= time_budget_ms
    return True, round(elapsed_ms, 3), "profiled-capacity"

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Memory Profile model · Theory Memory Profile, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Linear-time code automatically uses bounded memory.
